creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to ghislain from comment #7)

An hotel is, in my experience, extraordinary. 

I learnt (after being corrected, many years ago) to write the two words in that way, because some English speakers do pronounce hotel as beginning with a vowel. As the French would say, un hôtel.

I always write as if it begins with a vowel, and I always speak as if it begins with the consonant. Extraordinary.
